title: Common adjectives: choose the right word
lesson: Choose the adjective that describes each thing correctly. These are common words for size, feeling, and quality.

items.text: An elephant is a very {1} animal.
items.explanation: An elephant is big because of its great size.

items.text: A mouse is a {1} animal.
items.explanation: A mouse is small because it is little in size.

items.text: Be careful, the soup is very {1}!
items.explanation: Hot means having a high temperature.

items.text: In winter the water in the lake is {1}.
items.explanation: Cold means having a low temperature.

items.text: She is {1} because she got a nice present.
items.explanation: Happy means feeling good and pleased.

items.text: He is {1} because his dog is sick.
items.explanation: Sad means feeling unhappy.

items.text: My grandfather is {1}; he is ninety years old.
items.explanation: Old means having lived for many years.

items.text: I bought a {1} phone yesterday.
items.explanation: New means recently made or bought.

items.text: A racing car is very {1}.
items.explanation: Fast means moving at high speed.

items.text: A turtle is a {1} animal.
items.explanation: Slow means moving at low speed.

items.text: A basketball player is usually very {1}.
items.explanation: Tall means high from the ground to the top.

items.text: The little child is too {1} to reach the shelf.
items.explanation: Short means small in height.

items.text: This is a {1} film; I really enjoyed it.
items.explanation: Good means of high quality or pleasant.

items.text: The weather was {1}, so we stayed inside.
items.explanation: Bad means of low quality or not pleasant.

items.text: This test is {1}; I can answer every question.
items.explanation: Easy means simple to do.

items.text: This puzzle is too {1} for me to finish.
items.explanation: Hard means difficult to do.

items.text: That gold watch is very {1}.
items.explanation: Expensive means costing a lot of money.

items.text: These shoes were {1}; they only cost five pounds.
items.explanation: Cheap means costing little money.

items.text: After running all day, the boy was very {1}.
items.explanation: Tired means needing rest or sleep.

items.text: I have not eaten all day, so I am very {1}.
items.explanation: Hungry means wanting to eat food.

items.text: Please give me some water; I am so {1}.
items.explanation: Thirsty means wanting to drink.

items.text: The box is too {1} for me to lift.
items.explanation: Heavy means weighing a lot.

items.text: A feather is very {1}.
items.explanation: Light means weighing very little.

items.text: Please clean your shoes; they are very {1}.
items.explanation: Dirty means not clean.

items.text: The kitchen is {1} now because I washed everything.
items.explanation: Clean means free from dirt.

items.text: Lemons taste very {1}.
items.explanation: Sour is the sharp taste of a lemon.

items.text: Honey is very {1}.
items.explanation: Sweet is the taste of sugar or honey.

items.text: The river is very {1}, so you cannot touch the bottom.
items.explanation: Deep means going far down.

items.text: The road is very {1}, so two cars cannot pass.
items.explanation: Narrow means small from one side to the other.

items.text: The motorway is very {1}, with many lanes.
items.explanation: Wide means large from one side to the other.

items.text: The library is very {1}; nobody is talking.
items.explanation: Quiet means with little or no noise.

items.text: The music at the party was very {1}.
items.explanation: Loud means making a lot of noise.

items.text: She wears a {1} dress to the party.
items.explanation: Beautiful means very nice to look at.

items.text: The sun is very {1} today, so wear your hat.
items.explanation: Bright means giving a lot of light.
